theory.canonical_statement: 0ˆR \int_0ˆ{\pi} \int_0ˆ{2\pi} |B(r,t)|ˆ2/(2\mu_0) ; rˆ2 \sin\theta,dr d\theta d\phiUmag= $∫$ 0R $∫$ $0π∫02πB(r,t)2/(2µ0)r2sinθdrdθd.$ Canonical Registry Vol. 01 110 Trawin Zero Point Contextus Canonicus 2 TZP CANONICAL REGISTRY – ENTRIES 150–199 Mechanical compression of the dipole pair contributes a constraint energy Uconstraint=Umag+FmechdU_{\text{constraint}} = U_{\text{mag}} + F_{\text{mech}} dUconstraint=Umag+Fmechd, where the force between opposite dipoles separated by distance ddd is $Fmech=3µ0(µ1µ22(µ1d)(µ2d))/(2πd4)F_{\text{mech}}$ = 3\mu_0(\mu_1\cdot\mu_2 -2(\mu_1\cdot\hat{d})(\mu_2\cdot\hat{d}))/ (2\pi $d4)Fmech=3µ0(µ1µ22(µ1d)(µ2dˆ))/(2πd4).$ Interpretive Meaning: This quantifies the magnetic energy stored in the dipole configuration and the additional work required to maintain compression. The inverse fourthpower dependence of the force indicates strong sensitivity to separation distance. Dependencies: ID 318. Derivable
